items.definition_en: An anvil cloud is a flat-topped, spreading cloud at the top of a thunderstorm.
items.example_en: The anvil cloud signaled a strong thunderstorm approaching.

items.definition_en: A lenticular cloud is a lens-shaped cloud often formed over mountains.
items.example_en: Pilots keep an eye out for lenticular clouds near mountain ranges.

items.definition_en: A pileus cloud is a small, cap-shaped cloud that forms above a larger cloud.
items.example_en: A pileus cloud rested on top of the growing cumulus.

items.definition_en: A banner cloud trails from the downwind side of a mountain peak.
items.example_en: A banner cloud formed as strong winds hit the isolated summit.

items.definition_en: A cloud deck is a continuous large area of clouds with a fairly uniform base height.
items.example_en: The city was hidden under a thick cloud deck all day.

items.definition_en: A cloud formation is a distinctive shape or collection of clouds.
items.example_en: That strange cloud formation caught everyone's attention.

items.definition_en: The cloud ceiling refers to the lowest layer of clouds cover visible from the ground.
items.example_en: The pilots checked the cloud ceiling before takeoff.

items.definition_en: A roll cloud is a long, low, tube-shaped cloud that forms along the leading edge of thunderstorms.
items.example_en: The dramatic roll cloud stretched across the horizon.

items.definition_en: A mammatus cloud has puffy, pouch-like shapes hanging from the underside of another cloud.
items.example_en: Mammatus clouds appeared after the storm passed.

items.definition_en: A noctilucent cloud is a high cloud that glows at twilight.
items.example_en: We spotted a stunning noctilucent cloud over the horizon.

items.definition_en: A pancake cloud is a flat, round, stacked cloud layer.
items.example_en: The sky was filled with pancake clouds this morning.

items.definition_en: A fractus cloud is a small, ragged fragment of cloud that forms under larger clouds.
items.example_en: Jagged fractus clouds hung beneath the thunderstorm.

items.definition_en: A cloud plume is a narrow, trailing band of cloud from a source like a chimney or volcano.
items.example_en: A cloud plume rose from the factory after sunrise.

items.definition_en: A scud cloud is a low, fast-moving, ragged cloud usually seen under storm clouds.
items.example_en: Scud clouds raced across the sky before the rain began.

items.definition_en: A wave cloud forms in the air waves created by wind over mountains or hills.
items.example_en: Gliders look for wave clouds to find usable lift currents.

items.definition_en: A cloud wisp is a thin, delicate streak or fragment of cloud.
items.example_en: A few cloud wisps floated at high altitude.

items.definition_en: A cloud belt is a wide band of clouds stretching across the sky.
items.example_en: A thick cloud belt moved in from the west.

items.definition_en: Virga is rain or snow that falls from a cloud but evaporates before reaching the ground.
items.example_en: We could see the streaks of virga below the clouds.

items.definition_en: An undershot cloud forms as a low layer beneath a larger cloud mass.
items.example_en: Thunderstorms sometimes have an undershot cloud below the main cell.

items.definition_en: A cloud arc is a curved or bow-shaped cloud formation.
items.example_en: A faint cloud arc stretched above the treetops.
